rulings.Case Title: SHINGLE SPRINGS TRIBAL GAMING AUTHORITY et al VS. HOEM & ASSOCIATES, INC., A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses From Lakes Entertainment, Inc. To Hoem'S Request For Production Of Documents Set One Numbers 3-6, 10, 11, 14-16
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 4, 2012, Line 27. Motion To Compel Further Responses From Lakes Entertainment, Inc. To Hoem's Request For Production Of Documents Set One Numbers 3-6, 10, 11, 14-16. The court adopts in full the recommendation of Pro Tem Judge Watson as follows: Granted as to Request for Production of Documents, Set One, Requests Nos. 3-6, 10, 11, 14-16, specifically including all documents that Lakes Entertainment has agreed to produce, but has failed to provide. Monetary sanctions are denied. The court adds a production due date of December 21, 2012. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: FREDERICK THOMAS SEHER VS. GEORGE VALVERDE, DIRECTOR STATE OF CALIFORNIA,
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Mandate

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: ADVANCE INHERITANCE, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Why An Order For Sale Of Dwelling Should Not Be Issued To The Court
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 4, 2012, Line 9. Order To Show Cause Why An Order For Sale Of Dwelling Should Not Be Issued To The Court. Denied, without prejudice. In its application, Advanced Inheritance has not averred under oath: (1) whether or not the records of the county tax assessor indicate that there is a current homeowner's exemption or disabled veteran's exemption for the dwelling and the persons who claimed any such exemption (CCP § 704.760(a)); (2) whether the dwelling is a homestead and the amount of the homestead exemption, if any (CCP § 704.760(b)); and (3) the amount of any liens or encumbrances on the dwelling and the name and address of each person having a lien or encumbrance. (CCP § 704.760(c).) With the subsequent application, Advanced Inheritance shall submit as evidence an appraisal of the subject property and the calculations for the amount expected to be generated from the sale of Athena Whitley's interest in the property less any liens or encumbrances. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. Advanced Inheritance is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of its counsel in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/AJR)
